title: Fix per-run logfile creation (T-001)
summary: Makes the per-run logfile reliably exist after setup_logging() returns. Two-part fix: (1) ensure_parent + touch before the deduplication guard so the file is always created on disk; (2) make the deduplication guard path-specific (baseFilename comparison) so each unique run_log_path registers its own handler instead of being silently blocked by a prior test's handler.
why_safe: No trading logic changed — only logging setup
touch(exist_ok=True) is a no-op if file already exists, so production runs are unaffected
Guard change is strictly more correct: same path still deduplicates, different paths now register independently
No Birdeye or Jupiter calls involved — zero CU impact
No runtime/, state, journal, or position logic touched
expected_results: full_test_suite: 203+ passed (one fewer failure than freeze baseline of 202 passed / 5 failed)
commit_message:
fix: make per-run logfile exist synchronously after setup_logging() (T-001)

Fixes AUD-002. touch() the file before the deduplication guard so it
exists on disk regardless. Also makes the guard path-specific so test
isolation works correctly when multiple tests call setup_logging() with
different tmp_path values in the same pytest session.
notes_for_cursor: Do not include runtime/, logs/, .env, __pycache__, or any cache files
The change is lines 171-187 in utils.py (7 lines replaced with 12 lines)
